title: space_invite_set accepts Option<&SpaceInviteCreateInput> to support destroy-only calls without a separate method
description: space_invite_set takes input: Option<&SpaceInviteCreateInput>. When None, the create map serializes as JSON null, producing a valid destroy-only /set request. The alternative — two methods, one for create+destroy and one for destroy-only — doubles the surface area and splits related semantics. The alternative of always requiring an input and passing a sentinel struct is worse API ergonomics. JMAP /set is inherently a multi-operation request; making create optional on a single method matches the protocol model cleanly. Importantly, SpaceBan does NOT do this (it always requires input for create) because bans are never destroy-only in practice; the asymmetry is intentional.
